salient_text: subtree probe results with top fallback candidates
a focus_resolution_summary
if AXFocusedUIElement fails, it now tries:
frontmost app AXFocusedUIElement
frontmost app AXActiveElement

I also fixed the AX sibling focus comparison to use == instead of is while I was in there.

20260421-135931-785-conductor-axtextarea.source_summary: A Conductor chat response evaluating Blink's AX-based direction and saying the latest run cleared the main blocker. The reusable content is the long-term architecture guidance and the visible readiness-for-next-test checklist.
salient_text: For Blink specifically, I'd treat this as the right long-term direction:
use AX to identify target field, bounds, nearby structure, and sometimes caret
use event tap only as a signal that typing/focus activity happened
add AX observers later for focused element changed, selected text changed, and value changed
always tolerate partial AX exposure and degrade gracefully to field-bounds-level context
This last run shows the important blocker is cleared:
Gemini is producing a concrete output instead of NEEDS_REVIEW
So you're ready for another real test.

candidate_fields.value: For Blink specifically, the right long-term direction is to use AX to identify target field, bounds, nearby structure, and sometimes caret; use event tap only as a signal that typing or focus activity happened; add AX observers later for focused element changed, selected text changed, and value changed; and always tolerate partial AX exposure with field-bounds-level fallbacks.
candidate_fields.evidence: Visible architecture-guidance bullets

candidate_fields.value: This last run shows the important blocker is cleared: permissions are good, Conductor target focus is now being found, AX walk and geometry are working, and Gemini is producing a concrete output instead of NEEDS_REVIEW.
candidate_fields.evidence: Visible readiness summary and bullet list

style_notes: The message mixes architecture guidance with immediate testing advice.
uncertainties: The later best-next-test bullets extend below the fold.

20260421-140159-935-conductor-axtextarea.source_summary: A Google Doc draft for a YC application under the working title yc human app. The visible source contains a short company description, a fuller first-version product description centered on AI-powered copy-paste, example use cases, and a local open-source positioning statement.
salient_text: yc human app
Describe what your company does in 50 characters or less.
automatically move context between apps on your desktop.
What is your company going to make? Please describe your product and what it does or will do.
first version will be ai powered copy & paste
when user copies something, app takes screenshot of current window. when pasting, we use the source capture and target information to predict what the user wants to paste.

example use cases - filling out application fields from a resume pdf or google doc. copy and pasting from an image. relisting items on ecommerce platforms w/out having to switch between two tabs dozens of times.
everything can be deployed locally and is open source.
